## Global Instructions

For task tracking, work claiming, bd labels/metadata conventions, JSON
determinism, and multi-agent coordination, read
[beads](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/beads/.apm/context/beads.context.md).

This repo uses the Tauri MCP server to build, test, debug, and UI-drive a Tauri
v2 app. Before automating the UI, monitoring IPC, streaming logs, or writing
end-to-end tests, read [Tauri MCP context](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/mcp-tauri/.apm/context/tauri.tauri-mcp-index.context.md).

For the continuous commit/push working rhythm — atomic commits, pushing after
every meaningful step, and never stranding work in a local or disposable
worktree — read [delivery steering](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-delivery/.apm/context/delivery.delivery-index.context.md).

For branch discipline, merge-vs-PR choice, merge flags, and pre-push checks,
read [git workflow](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-git-workflow/.apm/context/git-workflow.git-workflow-index.context.md).

For communication style, epistemic defaults, and how to handle uncertainty,
tradeoffs, and brainstorming, read [pragmatic steering](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-pragmatic/.apm/context/pragmatic.pragmatic-index.context.md).

For repository layout, ownership boundaries, shared libraries, contracts, docs,
specs, tools, scripts, or product-agent assets, read
[project structure](apm_modules/srobroek/agentic-packages/packages/steering-project-structure/.apm/context/project-structure.project-structure-index.context.md).

# GitNexus — Code Intelligence

This project is indexed by GitNexus as **platevault** (30815 symbols, 63465 relationships, 265 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.

> Index stale? Run `node .gitnexus/run.cjs analyze` from the project root — it auto-selects an available runner. No `.gitnexus/run.cjs` yet? `npx gitnexus analyze` (npm 11 crash → `npm i -g gitnexus`; #1939).

## Always Do

- **MUST run impact analysis before editing any symbol.** Before modifying a function, class, or method, run `impact({target: "symbolName", direction: "upstream"})` and report the blast radius (direct callers, affected processes, risk level) to the user.
- **MUST run `detect_changes()` before committing** to verify your changes only affect expected symbols and execution flows. For regression review, compare against the default branch: `detect_changes({scope: "compare", base_ref: "main"})`.
- **MUST warn the user** if impact analysis returns HIGH or CRITICAL risk before proceeding with edits.
- When exploring unfamiliar code, use `query({search_query: "concept"})` to find execution flows instead of grepping. It returns process-grouped results ranked by relevance.
- When you need full context on a specific symbol — callers, callees, which execution flows it participates in — use `context({name: "symbolName"})`.
- For security review, `explain({target: "fileOrSymbol"})` lists taint findings (source→sink flows; needs `analyze --pdg`).

## Never Do

- NEVER edit a function, class, or method without first running `impact` on it.
- NEVER ignore HIGH or CRITICAL risk warnings from impact analysis.
- NEVER rename symbols with find-and-replace — use `rename` which understands the call graph.
- NEVER commit changes without running `detect_changes()` to check affected scope.
